---
title: "index"
author: "Assa Yeroslaviz"
date: '**last update** - `r format(Sys.time(), "%d %B, %Y")`'
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

The repository contains a conda-snakemake workflow for the mapping of the concatenated fastq file created at the BCF of the MPIB.


This README file for the sequencing center to help them run a preliminary analysis on the sequenced samples

The file will contains instruction on how to create a conda environment with the needed tools for the mapping and will also include snakemake for the automation of the scripts. 

## steps to do

**Preparing the genomes for mapping**

1. Download the genomes and the annotations from the FTP server (Ensembl genome builds are used here.)
2. For each genome, an index for the `star` aligner is created.

This two steps should be done only once, when the workflow was set up. It only needs to be re-run, when a new organism is being added, or when newer genome build of exiting organisms or a newer version of the STAR mapping tool are needed.

**Mapping step** 

3. Map the samples with STAR, bwa and/or bowtie2 (parameter-controlled).
4. Index the BAM files (`samtools index`).
5. Convert BAM files directly to bigWig using `deeptools bamCoverage`.

points to consider:
If this is only a preliminary analysis - 

1. Would it make more sense to take only 50% of the fastq file for a faster analysis / visualization
2. Do they need the bam files also - deleting them would save a lot of storage space

The examples in this document are based on fictive data as well as a fictional project named *Project0815*. When a new project is done, this term must be changed with the proper name of the project, wherever it is mentioned.

## folder structure

The workflow is controlled via `./scripts/bcfngs` and does not require changing directories for mapping.

* Mapping and run settings are saved in `configs/run.yaml`.
* Genome/index settings are saved in `configs/genomes.yaml`.
* The mapping step auto-detects FASTQ naming patterns (`*_R1.fastq.gz`, `*_R1_001.fastq.gz`, `*_L1_R1.fastq.gz`, `*.conc.R1.fastq.gz`).
* A unified mapping workflow handles both single-end and paired-end modes via command line flags.

## Workflow

The workflow uses a combination of a `conda` environment with the `snakemake` tool (based on python). The advantage of `conda` is that it creates a container, separated from the rest of the tools installed on the system. This gives a better management and control over the tools used in the workflow, as well as less conflict potentials with other/older version of tools with non-compatible parameters. To learn more about the conda environment and its advantages, take a look at the [conda web site](https://conda.io/en/latest/).

`snakemake` offers a lot of advantages as well. It allows better controls over different steps of the analysis, easier automation of the workflow and reduce running time through parallelization of the steps, just to mention some of them. to learn more about `snakemake` take a look at [the snakemake doc site](https://snakemake.readthedocs.io/en/stable/index.html).

- [environment](environment.html)
- [snakemake](snakemake.html)

### bigWig conversion

bigWig generation is performed with `deeptools bamCoverage` directly from BAM input.


TO DO

In this case it would probably be better if the indexed genomes would already be saved on the server for various organisms, as it doesn't make sense to download one each time. We sort of know which organisms are needed in most cases.

1. human
2. mouse
3. zebra fish
4. fruit fly
5. yeast
6. worm (C. elegans)

as well as which mappers we need

1. star
2. bwa
3. bowtie2

# CHANGES

### 04.03.2026

<<<<<<< Updated upstream
- Improved `./scripts/bcfngs genomes plan` preflight output:
  - Replaced the generic `OK` with a clear config validation message (`Config file is OK: ...`).
  - Replaced raw `HTTP/1.1 200 OK` header output from URL checks with readable per-organism status lines for FASTA and GTF URLs.
- Added version reporting in `genomes plan`:
  - Prints pinned genome build release(s), including Ensembl (currently 115 from config).
  - Prints configured tool versions for STAR, bwa, and bowtie2.
- Added Snakemake per-rule logging and environment declarations in `workflow/genomes.smk`:
  - Added `log:` targets for `download_sources`, `star_index`, `bwa_index`, `bowtie2_index`, and `chrom_size`.
  - Redirected rule command output to the corresponding log files.
  - Added `conda: "../env/environment.yml"` to those rules.
- Fixed follow-up issues introduced during logging changes:
  - Updated `download_sources` log path to include `{src_rel}` so output and log wildcards match.
  - Escaped shell block braces in `shell:` commands (`{{ ... }}`) to avoid Snakemake formatting errors.
=======
- Added unified mapping workflow (`workflow/mapping.smk`) with STAR, bwa, and bowtie2 support.
- Added wrapper commands:
  - `./scripts/bcfngs mapping plan`
  - `./scripts/bcfngs mapping run`
- Added paired-end/single-end mode as CLI flags (paired-end default).
- Replaced `bam2wig.py` conversion with `deeptools bamCoverage`.
- Added FASTQ pattern auto-detection for multiple naming schemes.
- Archived old STAR-only mapping Snakefiles under `archive/legacy/mapping/`.
- Added dedicated mapping documentation page (`mapping.qmd`).
>>>>>>> Stashed changes

### 20.02.2026

- Added Quarto website configuration (`_quarto.yml`) and re-rendered site with `quarto render`.
- Updated navbar:
  - Added links to [`environment`](environment.html), [`snakemake`](snakemake.html), and [`Updating genomes`](updating_genomes.html).
  - Replaced the old contact page with an email link: <mailto:yeroslaviz@biochem.mpg.de>.
  - Removed Twitter and added Bluesky.
- Converted `scripts/Updating_genomes.txt` into [`updating_genomes.qmd`](updating_genomes.qmd) (and removed the original text file).
- Switched environment management docs from conda to micromamba:
  - Added [`environment.qmd`](environment.qmd) and [`env/environment.yml`](env/environment.yml).
  - Added cookbook-style “how-to” instructions for common scenarios (new installs, updates, HPC module fallback).
- Added a new “one command” wrapper CLI: [`scripts/bcfngs`](scripts/bcfngs) (preflight checks, lint, dry-run, then run).
- Added new Snakemake configuration + workflow for genome download and indexing:
  - [`configs/genomes.yaml`](configs/genomes.yaml) (scientific organism IDs, pinned releases, index versioning)
  - [`configs/run.yaml`](configs/run.yaml) (per-project mapping settings)
  - [`workflow/genomes.smk`](workflow/genomes.smk) (downloads FASTA/GTF, builds STAR+bwa+bowtie2 indices, updates symlinks, creates chromSize)
- Updated the STAR mapping Snakefiles to use `configs/run.yaml` and scientific organism IDs.

### 27.06.2022

new genome built were added to the workflow

- _D. melanogaster_ - `BDGP6.28`
- _E. coli_ - `HUSEC2011CHR1`
- _D. pseudoobscura_ - `3.0.49`
- _C. elegans_ - `WBcel 2.35`

### 13.01.2020

- a short [`HOWTO`](HOWTO.html) was added. This serves as a fast introduction, if `conda` and `snakemake` are already installed and set for the mapping. 
- the genome of _D. pseudoobscura_ was added to the `config.yaml` file.

### 11.12.2019

The workflow now should work on the hpcl cluster of the MPI. For that reason some changes were made to the folder structure and the depth of the analysis

1. the mappers `bowtie2` and `bwa` were removed from the analysis. It will done from now on only with `STAR`.
2. the working directory of the analysis should be the `conc.fastq` folder in the pool-bcfngs pool folder of the Sequencing core facility.
3. The workflow will create a new folder with the structure `ProjectNumber/Organism/` were it will save two new folders for the star mapping results and the bigwig files.
4. the first script to get the genomes should be only run once, or when the genome version must be updated.
5. The second script was splitted into single-end and paired-end version and should be run accordingly.
